title: EACMS and IRA Authentication Failure Concentration

value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for NERC CIP compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes network, os and sourcetypes eacms:auth, eacms:auth`, linux:secure. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Normalize vendor syslog into `eacms:auth`. (2) Distinguish IRA accounts from vendors. (3) Integrate with ES adaptive response. (4) Retain raw events per records retention. Indexes required: `index=network`, `index=os`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=eacms:auth`, `sourcetype=eacms:auth``, `sourcetype=linux:secure`, `sourcetype=linux:secure``; Lookups: `eacms_asset_map`.
visualization: Table (top sources), Time chart (failures), Single value (locked-out systems). Key fields: `eacms_id`, `host`, `src_ip`, `user`.

references.title: Splunk Add-on for Unix and Linux
Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks

knownFalsePositives: ESP access from authorized engineering activities, vendor remote support windows, or scheduled audit observations.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Add-on for Unix and Linux (833), Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks (2757).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=network` `sourcetype=eacms:auth`, `index=os` `sourcetype=linux:secure`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Normalize vendor syslog into `eacms:auth`. (2) Distinguish IRA accounts from vendors. (3) Integrate with ES adaptive response. (4) Retain raw events per records retention.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
(index=network sourcetype=eacms:auth result="failure") OR (index=os sourcetype=linux:secure "Failed password")
earliest=-24h
| eval user=coalesce(user, account)
| lookup eacms_asset_map host OUTPUT eacms_id
| stats count by user, src_ip, host, eacms_id
| where count>25
| sort - count
```

Understanding this SPL

**EACMS and IRA Authentication Failure Concentration (CIP-005-6 R4 Part 4.2)** — We detect brute-force patterns against Electronic Access Control and Monitoring Systems that gate ESP remote access.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=network` `sourcetype=eacms:auth`, `index=os` `sourcetype=linux:secure`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for Unix and Linux (833), Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks (2757). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: network, os; **sourcetype**: eacms:auth, linux:secure. Those sourcetypes align with what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=network, index=os, sourcetype=eacms:auth, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **user** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by user, src_ip, host, eacms_id** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Filters the current rows with `where count>25` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the ESP or perimeter firewall log (for example Palo Alto or FortiGate) for the same window. Spot-check a few source events, reconcile counts, and verify field extractions, thresholds, and index permissions for the people who need this evidence.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (top sources), Time chart (failures), Single value (locked-out systems)
grandmaExplanation: We detect brute-force patterns against Electronic Access Control and Monitoring Systems that gate ESP remote access. That is important for the big power grid, where proof needs to line up with what the rules expect.
